summary: Russia launched a massive combined drone and missile attack on June 2, 2026, targeting Kyiv, Dnipro, Kharkiv, Zaporizhzhia, and energy infrastructure, causing power outages across seven regions and killing at least 7 people in the capital. Ukrainian forces retaliated with drone strikes deep into Russian territory, hitting an oil terminal in St. Petersburg, the Ilsky refinery in Krasnodar, and a bus in Yenakiyevo that killed 7 and injured 11. Ukrainian air defenses intercepted 11 of 33 Iskander ballistic missiles and 0 of 8 Zircon missiles, while Russia claimed to have shot down 354 Ukrainian drones overnight.
key_points: Massive Russian combined drone and missile strike hit Kyiv, Dnipro, Kharkiv, Zaporizhzhia; Ukroboronprom building on fire, power outages in 7 regions, 7 killed in Kyiv. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30605)
Ukrainian drones struck St. Petersburg oil terminal, Ilsky refinery in Krasnodar, and a Moscow-Simferopol bus in Yenakiyevo killing 7 and injuring 11. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30630)
Russia's MoD claimed 354 Ukrainian drones shot down overnight; Ukrainian air defenses hit 11 of 33 Iskander missiles and 0 of 8 Zircon missiles. (Source: @tass_agency/378739)
Russian forces advanced in Sumy direction up to 500m in 11 sections, fighting in Ivolzhansky and Pisarevsky; advancing toward Kachiy Lopan in Kharkiv direction. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30605)

Ukrainian drone threats tracked across Chernihiv, Dnipropetrovsk, Zaporizhzhia, Mykolaiv, Odesa, Zhytomyr, Sumy regions with multiple groups approaching from Kursk and Bryansk. (Source: @eRadarrua/95091)
High threat of Iskander-M ballistic missile launches from Bryansk region reported; 3 Shahed drones targeted Kyiv from the north. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67466)
Zelensky stated all Russian missile types require foreign components: 5 Kalibrs need 145 components, 33 Iskanders need 1,122, and 650 drones need over 17,000 components. (Source: @UkraineNow/81633)
93% of 1,066 inspected shelters in Ukraine found deficient; 80 shelters in critical condition unusable; worst situation in Kyiv, Lviv, Kharkiv, Zhytomyr regions. (Source: @UkraineNow/81626)

sections.executive_summary: On the night of June 2, 2026, Russia conducted a massive combined drone and missile attack on Ukrainian cities including Kyiv, Dnipro, Kharkiv, and Zaporizhzhia, targeting energy infrastructure and the Ukroboronprom defense enterprise, causing widespread power outages across seven regions and killing at least 7 people in Kyiv. Ukraine retaliated with long-range drone strikes against strategic Russian targets including the St. Petersburg oil terminal, the Ilsky refinery in Krasnodar, and a civilian bus in Yenakiyevo in Donetsk region. Russian forces continued ground advances in the Sumy and Kharkiv directions while Ukrainian air defenses struggled against the increased use of Iskander and Zircon ballistic missiles.

points: Ukrainian air defenses intercepted 11 of 33 Iskander ballistic missiles and 0 of 8 Zircon missiles during the June 2 attack; the enemy changed tactics by launching additional missiles in a morning wave after the main strike appeared to have ended. (Source: @UkraineNow/81612)

Ukrainian drones struck the St. Petersburg oil terminal, one of Russia's key oil product transshipment facilities in the Baltic region, on the same day as the city's economic forum. (Source: @UkraineNow/81638)

A Ukrainian drone struck a Moscow-Simferopol bus in Yenakiyevo, killing 7 people and injuring 11; drone-detectors subsequently identified reconnaissance UAVs over the crash site. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30630)

Power outages occurred in Kyiv and six regions (Kyiv, Donetsk, Dnipropetrovsk, Kharkiv, Sumy, Cherkasy oblasts) due to Russian strikes on energy infrastructure; five medical facilities in Kyiv were damaged or partially destroyed. (Source: @UkraineNow/81613)

President Zelensky stated Russian missiles cannot be produced without foreign components: 5 Kalibr missiles require 145 components, 33 Iskanders require 1,122 components, and 650 attack drones require over 17,000 components. (Source: @UkraineNow/81633)

93% of 1,066 inspected shelters across Ukraine had deficiencies; 80 shelters were in critical condition and unusable; the worst shelter situations were in Kyiv, Lviv, Kharkiv, and Zhytomyr regions. (Source: @UkraineNow/81626)

Multiple Ukrainian drone groups were tracked heading toward targets across Dnipropetrovsk, Zaporizhzhia, Mykolaiv, Odesa, Chernihiv, and Sumy regions, with threats from Kursk, Bryansk, and the Black Sea. (Source: @eRadarrua/95091)
Russia's Ministry of Defense claimed that 354 Ukrainian drones were shot down overnight across Russian regions during the June 1-2 period. (Source: @tass_agency/378739)
Seven Ukrainian drones were shot down over Vorongrad region overnight; two residential houses were damaged according to the regional governor. (Source: @tass_agency/378738)
Ukrainian drones attacked Michurinsk in Tambov region, damaging an apartment building, a library, and an art school according to the regional governor. (Source: @tass_agency/378718)
The Ilsky refinery in Krasnodar region caught fire due to a Ukrainian UAV attack; UAV debris also fell on a multi-story building in Slavyansk-on-Kuban. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30605)

In Kursk region, a civilian was killed when a Ukrainian drone hit a car in Shchekino village; three people were injured in Pushkarnoe village when a UAV attacked a car near a store; a man stepped on a mine in Novaya Nikolayevka. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30605)

In Belgorod region, a man was injured by shrapnel from an FPV drone attack on a car; multiple settlements including Dobrovoe, Novaya Tavolzhanka, Shebekino, and Ziborovka came under attack. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30605)

In Bryansk region, a Ukrainian FPV drone attacked a pig farm in Klimovsky district injuring a worker; two women were injured in Andreykovichi village; an electric train was attacked in Stara Guta injuring an assistant conductor. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30605)
In the Kharkiv direction, Russian assault units of the 'North' Group of Forces advanced toward the AFU logistics center at Kachiy Lopan; three AFU soldiers surrendered. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30605)
On the Volchansk section, Russian assault units of the 'North' Group of Forces advanced up to 400 meters and engaged in firefights in Okhrikovka village. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30605)
From Kupyansk, Russian forces expanded their zone of control in the built-up area despite increased Ukrainian UAV use. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30605)
In Konstantinovka, the situation for AFU further deteriorated in the built-up area despite increased Ukrainian aircraft use. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30605)

In the Dobropolsky direction, Russian forces operated in Mirnye, Vasilyevka, and Shevchenko settlements adjacent to Grishino, slowly advancing and consolidating the front line. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30605)
In eastern Zaporizhzhia region, Russian forces battled near KomsoMolskoe (Gulaypolskoe) village with the objective of reaching Orekhov. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30605)
Russian aerial bombs and missiles destroyed temporary Ukrainian Armed Forces deployment sites in Kharkiv and Zaporizhzhia regions. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30633)
In the Sumy direction, Russian units of the 'North' Group of Forces engaged in firefights in Bachivsk and surrounding areas in Shostka district. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30605)
In Sumy region, Russian forces advanced up to 11 sections of up to 500 meters and fought in Ivolzhansky and Pisarevsky. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30605)
Multiple Ukrainian drone groups crossed from Kursk region into Sumy region heading toward Terny; reconnaissance drones were detected near Kholmiv in Korukivskyi district. (Source: @eRadarrua/95132)
A high threat of Iskander-M ballistic missile launches from Bryansk region was reported; three Shahed drones approached Kyiv from the north. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67466)
Ukrainian drone threats were tracked toward Chernihiv region targets including Sosnytsia, Makoshyne, Khlmy, and Semenivka; a drone group headed toward Slavutych. (Source: @eRadarrua/95091)
A reactive drone threat was reported in northern Zhytomyr region heading toward Rivne region. (Source: @eRadarrua/95142)

In Dnipropetrovsk region, Ukrainian forces conducted offensive actions toward Alexandrovsk in the forest area beyond the Volchya River, consolidating and expanding the bridgehead north of the river. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30605)
Ukrainian drone groups were tracked across Dnipropetrovsk region heading toward Kryvyi Rih, Hirnytske, Nikopol, and Solone. (Source: @eRadarrua/95099)
In Zaporizhzhia region, drones were tracked near Ternuvate-Novomykolaivka and three drones near Kushuhum airport. (Source: @eRadarrua/95134)
Ukrainian drone groups from Kherson region headed toward Mykolaiv region targets including Snihurivka, Berezneguvate, Vodka, and Bashtanka. (Source: @kpszsu/63733)
Ukrainian drones from the Black Sea approached Odesa region toward Tatarbunary; drones were also tracked near Buialyk heading north. (Source: @kpszsu/63743)
Ukrainian strike drones targeted Chornomorsk in Odesa region. (Source: @eRadarrua/95099)

Russia launched 33 Iskander ballistic missiles and 8 Zircon missiles; Ukraine intercepted 11 Iskanders and 0 Zircons; Russia also deployed over 650 attack drones of various types. (Source: @UkraineNow/81612)
Russia's MoD claimed 354 Ukrainian drones were shot down overnight by air defense systems across multiple Russian regions. (Source: @tass_agency/378739)
Russian tactical aviation launched guided aviation bombs on Sumy region. (Source: @kpszsu/63711)

Russia changed attack tactics by launching a secondary missile wave in the morning after the main night strike, aiming to catch Ukrainian forces during reloading and repositioning. (Source: @UkraineNow/81612)
A high threat of Iskander-M ballistic missile launches from Bryansk region was issued; three Shahed drones were tracked approaching Kyiv from the north. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67466)

Ukrainian drones struck the Ilsky refinery in Krasnodar, the St. Petersburg oil terminal, and a bus in Yenakiyevo, demonstrating continued deep-strike capability into Russian territory. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30605)

key_developments: On June 2, 2026, Russia conducted a massive combined drone and missile attack targeting Kyiv, Dnipro, Kharkiv, and Zaporizhzhia, setting the Ukroboronprom building on fire and causing power outages in seven Ukrainian regions with at least 7 killed in Kyiv. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30605)

Ukrainian forces launched retaliatory drone strikes on strategic Russian targets including the St. Petersburg oil terminal, the Ilsky refinery in Krasnodar region, and a civilian bus in Yenakiyevo that killed 7 and injured 11 people. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30630)

Ukrainian air defenses intercepted only 11 of 33 Iskander ballistic missiles and 0 of 8 Zircon missiles; Russia changed tactics by launching a secondary morning missile wave to exploit Ukrainian force repositioning. (Source: @UkraineNow/81612)

Russia's MoD claimed 354 Ukrainian drones were shot down overnight across Russian territory; Ukrainian drones caused damage in Tambov, Voronezh, Krasnodar, and other regions. (Source: @tass_agency/378739)

Russian ground forces advanced in multiple sectors: up to 500m in 11 sections in Sumy direction, up to 400m at Volchansk, expanded control at Kupyansk, and continued operations near Konstantinovka and Dobropolye. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30605)

President Zelensky highlighted that all Russian missile production depends on foreign components, with 33 Iskanders requiring 1,122 components and 650 drones requiring over 17,000 components sourced through sanctions-circumvention schemes. (Source: @UkraineNow/81633)

A national shelter audit revealed 93% of 1,106 inspected facilities had deficiencies, with 80 shelters in critical unusable condition, posing severe civilian protection risks during ongoing mass attacks. (Source: @UkraineNow/81626)
sections.threat_assessment: The conflict is at a high escalation point with both sides conducting strategic strikes deep into each other's territory. Russia's increased use of ballistic missiles (Iskander and Zircon) combined with tactical changes—such as staggered attack waves—poses a serious challenge to Ukrainian air defenses, which demonstrated limited interception rates against these systems. Ukraine's continued ability to strike strategic targets in St. Petersburg, Krasnodar, and occupied Donetsk demonstrates sustained long-range strike capacity. The poor state of Ukrainian shelter infrastructure (93% deficient) represents a critical vulnerability for civilian protection during intensified attacks. Ground fighting remains active across the eastern and northern fronts with Russian forces making incremental advances in Sumy, Kharkiv, and Donetsk directions.
sections.regional_response: Former NATO Secretary General Stoltenberg stated that Europe is mentally unprepared for a major war despite recognizing the threat in its neighborhood, and characterized NATO's current situation as more complex than in 2024. Slovak President Peter Pellegrini stated that peace negotiations should be a prerequisite for discussing Ukraine's EU membership. The International Fencing Federation lifted sanctions against Russian and Belarusian athletes, allowing them to compete under their own flags starting with the 2026 World Senior Championships in Hong Kong. Poland's former ambassador to Ukraine returned his Ukrainian order citing the glorification of OUN-UPA figures.
sections.intelligence_notes: Russia's claim of shooting down 354 Ukrainian drones in one night represents an extraordinarily high figure that should be treated with caution and may include smaller commercial-grade UAVs. The tactical shift to staggered missile waves—launching a secondary strike during Ukrainian force repositioning—indicates Russian adaptation to Ukrainian air defense patterns. The targeting of a civilian bus in Yenakiyevo by Ukrainian forces raises questions about target identification and collateral damage assessment in occupied territory. The extensive drone threat tracking across multiple Ukrainian regions (Chernihiv, Dnipropetrovsk, Zaporizhzhia, Mykolaiv, Odesa, Sumy) indicates Ukraine maintains significant offensive drone capacity despite ongoing defensive demands. The shelter infrastructure crisis revealed by the ombudsman's report represents a systemic civilian protection failure that predates the current escalation.
